# Mira API

> Mira is an AI assistant platform with an OpenAI-compatible API. Built for the Russian market with pricing in rubles.

## Documentation

- [Introduction](https://platform.vmira.ai/docs/introduction): Overview of what Mira can do
- [Quickstart](https://platform.vmira.ai/docs/quickstart): Get started with the Mira API in minutes
- [Models](https://platform.vmira.ai/docs/models): Available models and their capabilities
- [Pricing](https://platform.vmira.ai/docs/pricing): Token pricing and subscription plans
- [Chat Completions API](https://platform.vmira.ai/docs/api/chat-completions): Main API endpoint reference
- [Authentication](https://platform.vmira.ai/docs/api/authentication): API key authentication
- [Streaming](https://platform.vmira.ai/docs/streaming): Server-sent events streaming
- [Tool Use](https://platform.vmira.ai/docs/tool-use): Function calling / tool use
- [Vision](https://platform.vmira.ai/docs/vision): Image understanding
- [Extended Thinking](https://platform.vmira.ai/docs/extended-thinking): Chain-of-thought reasoning
- [JSON Mode](https://platform.vmira.ai/docs/json-mode): Structured output
- [Embeddings](https://platform.vmira.ai/docs/embeddings): Text embeddings
- [Error Handling](https://platform.vmira.ai/docs/api/errors): Error codes and handling
- [Rate Limits](https://platform.vmira.ai/docs/api/rate-limits): Request rate limits
- [SDKs](https://platform.vmira.ai/docs/sdks): Official SDKs and libraries
- [Security](https://platform.vmira.ai/docs/security): Security best practices

## Base URL

    https://api.vmira.ai/v1

## Authentication

All requests require an API key in the Authorization header:

    Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY

## Models

| Model | Input (1K tokens) | Output (1K tokens) | Context |
|-------|-------------------|---------------------|---------|
| mira | 0.10 RUB | 0.30 RUB | 32K |
| mira-thinking | 0.15 RUB | 0.50 RUB | 32K |
| mira-pro | 0.30 RUB | 0.90 RUB | 64K |
| mira-max | 1.50 RUB | 6.00 RUB | 128K |

## Quick Example

```bash
curl https://api.vmira.ai/v1/chat/completions \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "model": "mira",
    "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}]
  }'
```

## OpenAI Compatibility

The Mira API is compatible with the OpenAI Chat Completions format. You can use the OpenAI SDK by changing the base URL:

```python
from openai import OpenAI
client = OpenAI(base_url="https://api.vmira.ai/v1", api_key="YOUR_API_KEY")
```

## Endpoints

- POST /v1/chat/completions - Create chat completion
- GET /v1/models - List available models
- POST /v1/embeddings - Create embeddings
